title: Spatial Cross Models
description: A resourcepack that makes Cross Models behave correctly in 3D space by flipping specific faces.
body:
> **_"You're never gonna unsee the issue in vanilla" - tuopi27_**

# About the pack
**Spatial Cross Models** is a resourcepack that fixes a visual bug in Minecraft, in which models containing flat planes have parts of their textures pop in and out of existence as you move around them.

By flipping and splitting specific faces and adding more model variations, it makes these models behave more naturally in 3D space and overall more realistically.

# How the Pack works
- The base model for all cross blocks, "cross.json", has both planes split into two parts vertically, and both parts of a plane use only one side of the blocks texture, creating a mirror effect when looking at the block from a diagonal angle, and showing the original texture when looking from the side.
- There is a new type of model for cross blocks called "directional_cross.json" for blocks that wouldn't work with the original method because of their texture (Poppy, Pale Oak Sapling), which only flips specific sides of the planes.
- There is also a new variant of the base cross.json file called the "no stretch cross" that removes the stretching effect that can sometimes occur with the base cross model.
- Some blocks that wouldn't look correct with any of the methods mentioned before and without any additional changes had their textures upscaled by 200% and shifted slightly (Tulips, Dead Bush)
- Other blocks that don't use the cross.json model, but had their models changed (Cactus, Dripleaf), have the changes baked-in directly into the blocks.

If you feel like this pack changes too much in-game but you still like the concept and would like to use it, there's also a smaller version of this pack called [Spatial Cross Models Lite](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/scm-lite) which contains less changes but keeps the most important ones.

# FAQ
**Q:** Will you backport the resourcepack to an older version, such as 1.XX?
> **A:** While it's not really supported, the pack should work on older versions without updating it. It's up to you to try and see!

**Q:** Is the pack compatible with other packs that change textures?
> **A:** Yes, although some blocks might look off due to the changes SCM makes.

# Additional Info

Compatible with the [Undead Bush](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/undead-bush) resourcepack.

Using this pack in a video or modpack is fine as long as proper credit is given.

If you wish to contact me you can do so via Gmail (happyfiedyt@gmail.com), Discord (@happyfied), or the [happyfied Modrinth Feedback Form](https://forms.gle/WRtN3YQVyJuZWpuN7).

Bugs wanted.
